description: ICLR topic subset (same values as papers.csv dataset_id)
UUID of the first paper presented to the judge. References papers.csv id.
UUID of the second paper presented to the judge. References papers.csv id.

UUID of the paper the judge selected as higher-impact. Always equals paper1_id or paper2_id (no ties allowed). Note: presentation order was randomly flipped with 50% probability before judging, so paper1/paper2 ordering does not indicate presentation order.
LLM provider: openai, anthropic, or gemini
Specific model: gpt-5.2, claude-opus-4-6, or gemini-3-pro-preview. Assigned via round-robin rotation. Judges run in standard mode (no extended thinking).
Judge's brief explanation of the decision (max ~150 words). The judge saw each paper's abstract (≤1500 chars) + Opus 4.6 Thinking impact assessment, NOT the full text or author names.
ISO 8601 timestamp of when the match was judged
